scopeBoundary: The repository engineering checker freezes the exact retained seven-package classification, runtime dependency declarations, workspace, Node and approved external imports, manifest loading fields, relative containment, recognized production-source module forms, and exact GitHub and verification-runner subprocess boundaries. It does not redefine dependency authority or grant product execution authority.

futureCheckClasses.description: Any future package, entry point, edge, source extension, or loading syntax requires an explicit Machine Task Contract and matching deterministic checker tests.
No inference package exists in the retained six-package surface. A future inference package requires explicit isolation checks before it can be classified.
The loading guard does not prove semantic absence of private Verdict, Evidence, Evidence Requirement, Policy, Rule, or Evidence Bundle definitions in delivery code.

partialEnforcementStatement: PRODUCT-MARKET-001 extends the bounded checker to the exact seven-package surface, approved YAML dependency, prototype entry point, verification-runner subprocess boundary, acyclic workspace graph, targeted shared-authority owner coupling, and declared entrypoint presence. The result remains a repository engineering drift guard, not general module resolution, transitive or generated-code analysis, operating-system sandbox proof, runtime data-flow analysis, inference-isolation proof, delivery-definition analysis, or complete architecture quality enforcement.
